governing_requirement: After a trusted producer invalidation, the Main consumer must idempotently tombstone exact affected cache and route scope, revalidate current authority before reuse, preserve immutable history, and restore the exact last compatible adopted release through a deterministic one-command rollback whose restart, stale-cache, revocation, and fault drills are independently verifiable.

ownership_boundary.MaskFactory: Publish the trusted invalidation and current producer state, then validate Main's signed cache, route, revalidation, and rollback evidence against the frozen contracts and exact producer heads.
ownership_boundary.Comfy_UI_Main: Own the consumer cache and route stores, apply invalidations, block affected DAG routes, revalidate adoption, install or roll back the consumer release, and sign consumer lifecycle evidence.
ownership_boundary.prohibited: MaskFactory must not implement, mutate, or sign Main's consumer cache, controller, route, adoption, or rollback state.

prerequisites: MF-P6-10.06 has no accepted production invalidation publisher/ledger.
MF-P6-10.01 through MF-P6-10.05 have no accepted published release, capability, consumer requirements, reproducible install, or live adoption decision.
The matching production Main consumer invalidation and rollback runtime has not published action evidence for conformance validation.
verified_foundations: The frozen mask_bridge_event contract contains cache_tombstoned, cache_rebuilt, adoption_revalidation_required, adoption_invalidated, rollback_started, and rollback_completed events.
The authoritative transition matrix assigns cache and rollback execution events to Comfy_UI_Main and rejects illegal producer/state combinations.

The signed lifecycle validator verifies schema, canonical payload hash, trusted consumer_journal signature, production key status, stream/epoch, contiguous sequence, prior hash, causation, event time, replayed state, expected head, and optional checkpoint binding.
Invalidation-driven release transitions must bind the exact signed producer invalidation ID and payload hash.

The architecture explicitly requires cache identity to include release, capability, source, request, ontology, policy, provider bundle, certificate, and revocation-index hashes, while historical evidence remains immutable.

commands: in-memory trusted consumer-journal cache tombstone/rebuild and rollback start/complete probes

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Create a correctly signed two-event consumer chain for artifact a*64 from active to tombstoned to active using only the frozen generic state_transition fields and release-snapshot payload identity.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: No exact cache key, route IDs, source/media scope, release/capability/request/ontology/policy/provider/certificate/revocation heads, tombstone receipt, current-state revalidation, or rebuilt-byte evidence is required.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Create a correctly signed two-event consumer chain for one release ID from none to rolling_back to rolled_back using only the generic state_transition and release-snapshot payload identity.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: No displaced release, target compatible adoption, installed-tree manifest, per-byte verification, active-pointer switch, rollback command identity, atomic receipt, restart recovery, or post-rollback route smoke is required.

confirmed_gaps: The frozen lifecycle events are signed declarations, not proof that the Main cache, routes, active release, or installed bytes changed.
No consumer action receipt resolves a producer invalidation's required action IDs to exact cache keys, routes, adoption scope, or completed results.
No deterministic cache-freshness decision recomputes current certificate, release, provider, ontology, policy, capability, and revocation state immediately before reuse.
No persistent consumer tombstone/revalidation ledger or restart reconstruction evidence is present in this producer repository.

No one-command consumer rollback evidence proves selection of the last compatible adopted release, exact installed-tree restoration, atomic pointer switch, retained history, and future-route exclusion of invalid scope.
No restart, stale-cache, revocation-during-use, crash-boundary, fork/reorder, or rollback drill has produced signed Main evidence that MaskFactory can validate.
The existing pipeline-stage cache is not the versioned bridge authority cache and receives no completion credit for this item.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive producer-side conformance verifier for signed Main cache/route invalidation, revalidation, and rollback evidence, without implementing or signing Main runtime state and without changing frozen v1 contracts.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. They are disjoint from preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, frozen v1 contracts, and prepared MF-P6-10.01 through 10.06 paths. Candidate paths are read-only.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_consumer_invalidation_conformance.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'cache or rollback or invalidation or revalidat'
acceptance_criteria: Consume only trusted signed current producer invalidation/publication state and trusted signed Main consumer evidence; never derive consumer action success from a declared lifecycle event alone.
Bind every producer required-action ID to exactly one completed Main action result covering the exact target transition, cache keys, routes, adoption scope, and pre/post state hashes.

Require exact cache identity and freshness evidence over release, capability, source, request, ontology, policy, provider bundle, certificate, and revocation head; stale or ambiguous reuse fails closed.
Require tombstoned scope to be absent from future production route decisions while immutable historical bytes and audit records remain readable.

Require revalidation to observe current producer state and restore only exact eligible cache/routes; caller assertions cannot override certificate, release, provider, policy, ontology, capability, or revocation drift.

Require rollback to the exact last compatible adopted release with published byte catalog, prior/target install manifests, atomic active-pointer transaction, per-file verification, signed receipt-last evidence, and post-switch route/API smokes.
Validate restart, stale-cache, revocation-during-use, crash-at-every-durable-boundary, fork/reorder, concurrent invalidation, idempotent replay, and same-key/different-body fault evidence.

Emit a strict self-hashed producer conformance decision that records exact producer and Main journal heads, release/adoption identities, observed evidence hashes, coverage, verdict, and deterministic blockers.
Frozen v1 schemas/fixtures, prepared/queued worker records, and preserved MF-P6-08.05 paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits only the frozen event vocabulary and signed lifecycle-chain validator. It does not prove that Main applied any invalidation, changed a cache or route, revalidated adoption, installed or rolled back a release, passed a recovery drill, or completed MF-P6-10.07.
